ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Jun-06-2018 Granted Per Written Order

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Aug-15-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Amanda Ebey for petitioner Alice Nora.

Ms. Ebey informed the Court that although the estate was modest in size, it had required extensive litigation to bring it to the stage of distribution. She stated that a forged deed had resulted in transfer of title, and that litigation had been necessary to recover the home and restore title to the decedent. Further, the daughter of the decedent's former wife had also claimed an interest in the property, and litigation had been required to defend against that claim as well. Ms. Ebey added that she was expecting to receive a supplemental tax bill from the City and County of San Francisco, but was unaware of the amount of additional taxes which would be due. The matter was taken under submission.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Craig Blackstone. Bailiff: Deputy Nuque. (Not reported)

Off Calendar Jan-25-2016 Continued To Feb-22-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Amanda Ebey for and with Alice Nora (via court call).

Ms. Ebey reported that they filed a status report, that the Chase Bank receipt has been filed, that the balance was to go into an account at Wells Fargo but they found it would not be an interest bearing account, that they have filed an ex parte petition to waive the requirement of interest in this account and that currently the funds are still with the title company (Cornerstone). The Court granted the ex parte petition for order authorizing deposit into blocked non-interest bearing account at Well Fargo Bank (subject to review of the order). The status on filing receipts for funds into a blocked account was continued to February 22, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Order to Appear is off calendar.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Kabasares. (Not Reported)

Off Calendar Jan-05-2016 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

The Court stated that we are here for status of filing receipts into a blocked account and that the receipts have not been filed. The status hearing is also continued to that date.

Petition To Determine Interest In Proceeds From Sale Of Real Property
Off Calendar Dec-07-2015 Continued To Apr-11-16 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Amanda Ebey for Alice Nora; John Cullen for Lottie Mayfield.

Ms. Ebey informed the Court that she had spent two hours in discussions with banks to get the receipts, which effort is still in progress. She stated that anticipated having the receipts on file within two weeks. The Court continued the status hearing re receipts to blocked account to January 4, 2016 at 9:00 am in Department 204.
Mr. Cullen stated that with respect to the remaining dispute, the parties needed a trial date, and requested that the matter be set for trial in March of 2016. Ms. Ebey responded that she needed a trial date in April. The Court set the matter for trial on April 11, 2016, at 9:00 am in Department 206. Mr. Cullen stated that the case was suitable for mediation. Ms. Ebey replied that she was agreeable to mediation. The Court set a deadline of March 21, 2016 for completion of mediation.
Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Craig Blackstone. Bailiff: Deputy Kabasares. (Not reported).

Off Calendar Nov-03-2015 Continued To Jan-05-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

The Court stated that there is no appearance, that we are here on a status of close of estate and status of filing receipts for funds into a blocked account, that an 850 petition has been filed and that the estate will need to remain open for that. An Order to Appear will be issued to the Administrator Alice Nora and the Attorney Amanda Ebey to appear on December 1, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to explain why the receipt for funds deposited into a blocked account has not been filed. The status hearing is also continued to that date. The status on filing a petition for final distribution was continued to January 5, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Tim Curran, realtor for the estate, was present and stated that he had expected Ms. Ebey to be present, that Ms. Nora lives in Louisiana, that he spoke with her yesterday and told her he would give her the disposition from today's hearing, confirmed that escrow has closed and that the funds are sitting at the title company.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Kabasares. (Not Reported)

REPORT OF SALE AND PETITION TO CONFIRM SALE OF REAL PROPERTY (1537 Revere Avenue, San Francisco Ca 94124)
Off Calendar Sep-11-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Amanda Ebey for (Administrator) Alice Nora.

Ms. Ebey stated that the parties here to bid need to be aware that the property is being sold with the term that the buyer is responsible for removing all of the personal property and debris from the property. The sale was confirmed at $525,000.00 based on the terms and conditions as set forth in the report of sale. The Court granted the Petition to Confirm Sale of Real Property (including the term indicated above) (subject to receipt/review of the order). The funds will be deposited into a blocked account at Citibank in Louisiana, with any amount over $250,000.00 being deposited into a second bank in the same location. Status on filing receipts for deposit of funds into the blocked accounts is set for September 23, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Kabasares. (Not Reported)

REPORT OF SALE AND PETITION TO CONFIRM SALE OF REAL PROPERTY (1537 Revere Avenue, San Francisco Ca 94124)
Off Calendar Aug-31-2015 Continued To Sep-09-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Amanda Ebey for Alice Nora.

Ms. Ebey reported that the realtor has a good relationship with the potential buyer, that there are bids coming in, that they request that proceeds of the sale remain in escrow until they receive an order for final distribution, that distribution will only be to Ms. Nora and if that is not possible would prefer to put the funds into a blocked account instead of obtaining a bond for such a short period of time. The report of sale and petition to confirm sale of real property was continued to September 9, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Kabasares. (Not Reported)

REPORT OF SALE AND PETITION TO CONFIRM SALE OF REAL PROPERTY (1537 Revere Avenue, San Francisco Ca 94124)
Off Calendar Aug-10-2015 Continued To Aug-31-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Amanda Ebey for Petitioner Alice Nora
The Court stated that the sale was not ready to proceed as there was no appraisal on file. Ms. Ebey replied that she wanted a three week continuance to get the appraisal on file and the petition renoticed. The matter was continued to August 31, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.
Judge: Paul H. Alvarado. Clerk: Craig Blackstone. Bailiff: Deputy Kabasares.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Filing Inventory & Appraisal; Petition For Final Distribution Or Petition For Approval Of Report On Status Of Administration
Off Calendar Jun-29-2015 Continued To Sep-28-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: The following matters were heard together: PES-05-287669 Estate of William Sykes & PES-07-289966 Estate of Maggie Lee Sykes.
Appearances: Amanda Ebey for Alice Nora.

Ms. Ebey reported (re 287669) that it is an ancillary administration, that they are planning to sell the property, that there are tax liens but nothing will be done until April of 2016 and that they want to get this closed out. Ms. Ebey stated that she will look into the Maggie Sykes estate but that she did not know anything about it. The court stated that it had issued an Order to Appear and that it was open to recommendation regarding what to do with the current attorney and Ms. Mayfield. Ms. Ebey stated that she assumes that they have dropped to matter and that she will look into it. The status of filing an Inventory & Appraisal and petition for final distribution or petition for approval of report on status of administration (in both cases) and the Order to Appear (in case 289966) were continued to September 28, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez/Craig Blackstone. Bailiff: Deputy Allen. (Not Reported)

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Filing Inventory & Appraisal; Petition For Final Distribution Or Petition For Approval Of Report On Status Of Administration
Off Calendar Apr-20-2015 Continued To Jun-29-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: The following cases were heard: PES-05-287669 Estate of William Sykes & PES-07-289966 Estate of Maggie Sykes. ***
Appearances: Alice Nora (via Court Call) in Propria Persona.

The Court asked Ms. Nora as to the status of Mr. Rice and she answered that he was deceased. The Court stated that it would continue the matter for Ms. Nora to obtain counsel who can respond to the examiner's notes and to appear at the next hearing. The status of filing inventory & appraisal, etc. was continued to June 29, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

***Subsequent to the hearing, it was brought to the court's attention by Tim Curran that Ms. Nora is not associated with case PES-07-289966. See that case for minutes. Mr. Curran also stated that he will be assisting Ms. Nora with the sale of the property, that a fraudulent lien had been taken out, that the property is worth $300,000.00 and that after costs there will be sufficient funds to pay an attorney.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Allen. (Not Reported)

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar Dec-15-2008 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Leroy Rice for the Estate of William Sykes. Terry Mollica for HSBC Bank (Interested Party). John Cullen for Lottie Mayfield (Petitioner).

The Court questioned why this will (1991) was not being probated in Louisiana. The parties presented argument to support their positions. Upon the conclusion of arguments, the Court denied the petition stating that the domiciliary court is Louisiana and the matter should be determined by Louisiana. Mr. Cullen was directed to prepare the order.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Katy Bersamin, CSR#11722. Clerk: J. Alameda/R. Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. Harris.

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar Dec-09-2008 Continued To Dec-15-08 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Determination Of Interests In Real Property Or Proceeds From Sale, If Any, Of Said Property
Off Calendar Dec-08-2008 Denied
MINI MINUTES: This matter was called with case number 289966.

Appearances: John Cullen for Lottie Mayfield/Estate of Maggie Sykes. Terry Mollica for HSBC (Interested Party). Leroy Rice for the Estate of William Sykes.

The Court stated it had read the petition and was aware of the pending civil action. The parties presented argument to support their positions. At the conclusion of argument, the Court stated it believed the matter was before Judge Mellon and referenced page four of Judge Mellon's statement of decision in case number 445996. The Court denied both petitions without prejudice. Mr. Cullen to prepare the order.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Arlene Howell, CSR#2542. Clerk: J. Alameda./R. Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. Harris.
